zh.Levens, Westmorland, England: 英國維斯特莫藍德列文斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Matthew Redman於大約1190年出生在英國越可爾時，他的父親Sir Henry Redmayne, I27歲，母親Margretha Adam22歲。他和Amabel至少有4個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1254年之前在英國維斯特莫藍德。

zh-hans.Levens, Westmorland, England: 英国維斯特莫藍德列文斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Matthew Redman于大约1190年出生在英国越可爾时，他的父亲Sir Henry Redmayne, I27岁，母亲Margretha Adam22岁。他和Amabel至少有4个儿子和2个女儿。他去世于1254年之前在英国維斯特莫藍德。

nameMeanings.name: Redman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English red (Old English hrēod) ‘reed’ + -man, presumably for a thatcher or reed-cutter. Compare Reeder .
English: in southeastern England sometimes a topographic name perhaps for someone who lived by a clearing, from Middle English rede (Old English rēod, rȳd) ‘clearing’ + man.
English: habitational name from Redmain (Cumberland), from Old English rēad ‘red’ + an uncertain second element, perhaps a dialect form of man ‘cairn’.

variants.name: Reeder
meanings: English: occupational name for someone who used reeds as a roofing material (a thatcher), a derivative of Middle English rede ‘reed’ + -er. Compare Reed and Redman .

English: topographic name for someone who lived by a clearing, a derivative of Middle English rede, ride, rude ‘clearing’ (see Reed ) + -er, synonymous and interchangeable with atte Rede, atte Rude ‘at the clearing’. This type of name, composed of a topographic term + -er, is common in Kent, Surrey and Sussex, and it is also found in Essex.
Dutch and German: variant of   Reder   3.

variants.name: Redhead
meanings: nickname for someone with red hair, from Middle English red ‘red’ + he(ve)d ‘head’. Compare Redman .
habitational name from an unidentified place in northern England called Redhead, probably meaning ‘red headland’.

variants.name: Rodman
meanings: English: occupational name for a thatcher or reed-cutter, from Southwest Middle English rod(e), rud(e) (Old English hrēod) ‘reed’ + -man. Synonymous with Redman 1.
English: topographic name for someone who lived at a clearing, from Middle English rode (Old English rodu) or Southwest Middle English rude (Old English rȳde) ‘clearing’ + man. See Redman 2.
Americanized form of German Rodmann: variant of Rodemann (see Rodeman ).

nameMeanings.name: Matthew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name of the Christian evangelist, author of the first gospel in the New Testament. His name is a form of the Hebrew name Mattathia, meaning ‘gift of God’, which is fairly common in the Old Testament, being rendered in the Authorized Version in a number of different forms: Mattan(i)ah, Mattatha(h), Mattithiah, Mattathias, and so on. In the Authorized Version, the evangelist is regularly referred to as Matthew, while the apostle chosen to replace Judas Iscariot is distinguished as Matthias . A related name from the same Hebrew roots, but reversed, is Jonathan . Throughout the English-speaking world Matthew has been particularly popular since the 1970s.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Matthew Redman was born about 1190, in Yorkshire, England, his father, Sir Henry Redmayne, I, was 27 and his mother, Margretha Adam, was 22. He had at least 4 sons and 2 daughters with Amabel. He died before 1254, in Westmorland, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Matthew Redman est né environ 1190, à Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Sir Henry Redmayne, I, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Margretha Adam, avait 22 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles avec Amabel. Il est décédé avant 1254, à Westmorland, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Matthew Redman um 1190 in Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Henry Redmayne, I 27 und seine Mutter Margretha Adam 22. Er hatte mit Amabel mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb vor 1254 in Westmorland, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Matthew Redman è nato il circa 1190 nel Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, Sir Henry Redmayne, I, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Margretha Adam, aveva 22 anni. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie con Amabel. Morì prima del 1254, nel Westmorland, England.

ja.Yorkshire, England: イギリスヨークシュアイア
ja.Lancaster, Lancashire, England: イギリスランカシュアイアランカスター
ja.Westmorland, England: イギリスウエストモーランド
ja.Levens, Westmorland, England: イギリスウエストモーランドレベンス
ja.lifeSummary: Matthew Redmanが約1190年にイギリスヨークシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir Henry Redmayne, Iは27歳，母Margretha Adamは22歳であった。彼はAmabelとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1254年以前にイギリスウエストモーランドで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Matthew Redman(이)가 대략 1190년 영국 요르크실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Henry Redmayne, I(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Margretha Adam(은)는 22세였다. 그는 Amabel(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1254년 이전 영국 웨스트모를랜드에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Matthew Redman nasceu aproximadamente 1190, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Henry Redmayne, I, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Margretha Adam, tinha 22 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas com Amabel. Ele faleceu antes 1254, em Westmorland, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Matthew Redman родился около 1190 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Henry Redmayne, I было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Margretha Adam, 22. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери от Amabel. Он умер перед 1254 г. в местности вестморланд, Англия.

sm.Abt 1190–before 1254: e tusa o le 1190–a'e le'I oo le 1254

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Matthew Redman nació aproximadamente 1190, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Henry Redmayne, I, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Margretha Adam, tenía 22 años. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas con Amabel. Murió antes de 1254, en Westmorland, Inglaterra.
